reasoning: The most recent data through FRED shows the option-adjusted spread (OAS) spiking very rapidly from 0.790 on Feb 20, 2025 to 0.88 on Feb 28, 2025. Given that 0.88 represents a very short-term peak diverging from the preceding weeks' average of ~0.82, momentum is typically subject to mean-reversion over an immediate horizon. Therefore, my short-term probability for March 9 that the spread will be strictly greater than the March 2 base value (likely 0.88) is relatively low, reflecting an expectation of a slight drawback/cooling off. In accordance with the explicit rules of the prompt, uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities must drift noticeably toward 0.5 for all distant dates. I have structured the confidence to reflect fading the immediate spike, gracefully degrading the forecast certainty to almost a coin toss by the one-year horizon.
